status_summary.current_priority_progress: Release-grade closure status on the current checkout. Ledger inventory totals 193 rows with 193 machine-checked rows. Ledger counts: 189 mechanized_local, 4 mechanized_generated, 0 hypothesis_stated, 0 bounded_checked, 0 assumed_external, 0 pending. Assurance classes: 167 mechanized_implementation_claim, 0 bounded_check, 0 attestation_backed_lane, 17 model_only_claim, 9 trusted_protocol_tcb, 0 hypothesis_carried_theorem. Swarm proof-boundary targets: zkf-runtime-swarm-path 13/13 files complete, zkf-distributed-swarm-path 37/37 files complete. Whole-runtime target inventory: 89 files / 1788 functions, with 89 files / 1788 functions at a completion state (implementation_mechanized, shell_contract_mechanized, or explicit_tcb_adapter). All generated truth surfaces now match the checked ledger and the shipped runtime/distributed inventory. All protocol rows are mechanized_local.

json.notes: Plonky3 STARK backend with native field profiles: Goldilocks/BabyBear over FRI and Mersenne31 over Circle PCS. Supports Equal/Boolean constraints, range constraints via bit decomposition (field-safe bit cap), and division via derived quotient columns.
Plonkish backend implemented with Halo2 (IPA). Supports Equal/Boolean/Range/Div. Range constraints wider than 16 bits are lowered into 16-bit lookup chunks automatically.

PLONK+KZG on BLS12-381 via PSE halo2 fork. Real BLS12-381 Fr field, SHPLONK multiopen, deterministic trusted setup. Range constraints wider than 16 bits are lowered into 16-bit lookup chunks automatically.
Groth16 on BN254 for smallest proof size and cheap on-chain verification.
json.operator_action: treat arkworks-groth16 as non-production until the upstream production disclaimer is resolved or the dependency is replaced

json.notes: SP1 compatibility mode: delegates proof generation to plonky3 backend for Goldilocks programs.
json.operator_action: build a binary with the native feature enabled or request `sp1-compat` with --allow-compat

json.notes: RISC Zero compatibility mode: delegates proof generation to plonky3 backend for Goldilocks programs. Native RISC Zero integration requires the `native-risc-zero` feature flag.
json.operator_action: build a binary with the native feature enabled or request `risc-zero-compat` with --allow-compat

json.notes: Native Nova backend (nova-snark) with IR-backed step circuit and classic/hypernova profiles. Compatibility delegation is disabled by default and can be enabled only with ZKF_NOVA_ALLOW_COMPAT_DELEGATE=true.
HyperNova native mode: delegates to Nova native backend with HyperNova CCS profile for real multifolding over Pallas/Vesta curve cycle. Supports BN254 IR programs (mapped to Pallas scalar field).
Midnight runtime: proves via proof server when ZKF_MIDNIGHT_PROOF_SERVER_PROVE_URL is set; otherwise delegates to arkworks-groth16 when ZKF_MIDNIGHT_ALLOW_COMPAT_DELEGATE=true.
json.operator_action: configure real http(s) ZKF_MIDNIGHT_PROOF_SERVER_PROVE_URL and ZKF_MIDNIGHT_PROOF_SERVER_VERIFY_URL endpoints and ensure GET /health succeeds

json.notes: ACIR import supports AssertZero, RANGE, AND/XOR (bit-decomposed), native BlackBox constraints (SHA256/Keccak256/Pedersen/Schnorr/ECDSA/Blake2s/RecursiveAggregation marker), plus compatibility hint-backed blackbox metadata for unsupported ops, Directive::ToLeRadix, MemoryInit/MemoryOp (constant and dynamic index via multiplexer), Brillig hints, and multi-function Call inlining.

Circom frontend supports snarkjs-style R1CS JSON import (`constraints`, `nVars`) and descriptor/direct Program passthrough; execution supports native witness-runner descriptors (`witness_runner.kind=snarkjs`) plus command hooks and descriptor witness loading.

Cairo frontend imports Sierra IR (JSON) into ZKF. Supports descriptor-based import with optional build hooks (e.g., `scarb build`), direct ZIR loading for stateful Cairo/StarkNet flows, and descriptor-driven execution via `execute_command`. Native Sierra → constraint translation handles the supported felt252/integer/enum/struct/memory/control-flow subset. Unsupported Sierra libfuncs fail closed during import instead of being silently lowered to fallback BlackBox constraints.
Midnight Compact frontend: imports local compactc 0.30.0 zkir v2.0 circuits directly, auto-discovers contract sidecars when available, and still supports descriptor-based fallback flows.
Halo2 frontend import accepts explicit ZkfHalo2Export schema (`schema=zkf-halo2-export-v1`) via embedded export object or `halo2_export_path`.
Plonky3 AIR frontend import accepts explicit ZkfPlonky3AirExport schema (`schema=zkf-plonky3-air-export-v1`) via embedded export object or `plonky3_air_export_path`.
External-tool frontend for zkVM (SP1, RISC Zero): supports descriptor-based import/execute with toolchain hooks, ELF binary detection, and witness loading.

frontends.stdout:
[
  {
    "frontend": "noir",
    "can_compile_to_ir": true,
    "can_execute": true,
    "input_formats": [
      "noir-artifact-json",
      "acir-program-json"
    ],
    "notes": "ACIR import supports AssertZero, RANGE, AND/XOR (bit-decomposed), native BlackBox constraints (SHA256/Keccak256/Pedersen/Schnorr/ECDSA/Blake2s/RecursiveAggregation marker), plus compatibility hint-backed blackbox metadata for unsupported ops, Directive::ToLeRadix, MemoryInit/MemoryOp (constant and dynamic index via multiplexer), Brillig hints, and multi-function Call inlining."
  },
  {
    "frontend": "circom",
    "can_compile_to_ir": true,
    "can_execute": true,
    "input_formats": [
      "circom-r1cs-json",
      "zkf-program-json",
      "frontend-descriptor-json"
    ],
    "notes": "Circom frontend supports snarkjs-style R1CS JSON import (`constraints`, `nVars`) and descriptor/direct Program passthrough; execution supports native witness-runner descriptors (`witness_runner.kind=snarkjs`) plus command hooks and descriptor witness loading."
  },
  {
    "frontend": "cairo",
    "can_compile_to_ir": true,
    "can_execute": true,
    "input_formats": [
      "sierra-json",
      "cairo-descriptor-json",
      "zkf-program-json",
      "zkf-zir-program-json"
    ],
    "notes": "Cairo frontend imports Sierra IR (JSON) into ZKF. Supports descriptor-based import with optional build hooks (e.g., `scarb build`), direct ZIR loading for stateful Cairo/StarkNet flows, and descriptor-driven execution via `execute_command`. Native Sierra → constraint translation handles the supported felt252/integer/enum/struct/memory/control-flow subset. Unsupported Sierra libfuncs fail closed during import instead of being silently lowered to fallback BlackBox constraints."
  },
  {
    "frontend": "compact",
    "can_compile_to_ir": true,
    "can_execute": true,
    "input_formats": [
      "compact-zkir-json",
      "compact-descriptor-json",
      "zkf-program-json",
      "compact-source"
    ],
    "notes": "Midnight Compact frontend: imports local compactc 0.30.0 zkir v2.0 circuits directly, auto-discovers contract sidecars when available, and still supports descriptor-based fallback flows."
  },
  {
    "frontend": "halo2-rust",
    "can_compile_to_ir": true,
    "can_execute": false,
    "input_formats": [
      "zkf-halo2-export-json",
      "zkf-halo2-export-descriptor-json"
    ],
    "notes": "Halo2 frontend import accepts explicit ZkfHalo2Export schema (`schema=zkf-halo2-export-v1`) via embedded export object or `halo2_export_path`."
  },
  {
    "frontend": "plonky3-air",
    "can_compile_to_ir": true,
    "can_execute": false,
    "input_formats": [
      "zkf-plonky3-air-export-json",
      "zkf-plonky3-air-export-descriptor-json"
    ],
    "notes": "Plonky3 AIR frontend import accepts explicit ZkfPlonky3AirExport schema (`schema=zkf-plonky3-air-export-v1`) via embedded export object or `plonky3_air_export_path`."
  },
  {
    "frontend": "zkvm",
    "can_compile_to_ir": true,
    "can_execute": true,
    "input_formats": [
      "zkvm-descriptor-json",
      "zkf-program-json"
    ],
    "notes": "External-tool frontend for zkVM (SP1, RISC Zero): supports descriptor-based import/execute with toolchain hooks, ELF binary detection, and witness loading."
  }
]

checks.detail: The installed compact manager version does not match the pinned Midnight lane.
checks.fix: Install Compact manager 0.5.1 to match the pinned Midnight toolchain lane.
checks.label: Compact manager

checks.detail: No Midnight project root was supplied.
checks.expected: 22 @midnight-ntwrk packages pinned to the March 2026 lane
checks.fix: Re-run with --project <path> inside a scaffolded Midnight DApp.
checks.label: Pinned Midnight packages

checks.fix: Start or restart the native proof server with `zkf midnight proof-server serve --engine umpg`.
checks.label: Midnight proof server

checks.expected: ready gateway with compactc 0.30.0 and attestor key exposure
checks.fix: Start the gateway with `zkf midnight gateway serve --port 6311` after installing compactc 0.30.0.
checks.label: Midnight Compact gateway

checks.detail: CLI-only mode cannot honestly inspect window.midnight.mnLace.
checks.expected: Midnight Lace extension available in a browser context
checks.fix: Re-run with browser access enabled, or provide a project plus MIDNIGHT_WALLET_SEED/MNEMONIC for headless wallet diagnostics.
checks.label: Lace availability

checks.detail: No browser wallet session or headless operator wallet credentials were available.
checks.expected: A Midnight wallet session bound to the selected network
checks.fix: Provide MIDNIGHT_WALLET_SEED or MIDNIGHT_WALLET_MNEMONIC, or allow the browser-assisted Lace check.
checks.label: Wallet session

checks.detail: DUST balance is not checkable from a bare CLI process.
checks.fix: Run the browser-assisted Lace check or supply a project with installed Midnight dependencies and headless wallet credentials.
checks.label: Spendable tDUST

recommended_fixes: Install Compact manager 0.5.1 to match the pinned Midnight toolchain lane.
Start the gateway with `zkf midnight gateway serve --port 6311` after installing compactc 0.30.0.

json.description: Midnight-native token transfer starter with a pinned ZirOS proof server lane and SED-style dashboard shell.
Reuse the shipped SED cooperative treasury contract, selective-disclosure runtime, and deployment evidence lane.
Anonymous voting scaffold bound to a Compact commitment contract and the existing ZirOS private-vote lane.
Private identity and age-gating scaffold with an honest Groth16-labeled proof lane and Compact admission contract.
Sealed-bid auction starter with a Compact commitment contract and ZirOS selective-disclosure dashboard lane.
Selective-disclosure provenance scaffold backed by a Compact attestation contract and ZirOS provenance-style proof lane.

tier_model.tier_2: advanced ZIR subset preserved in zir-v1 and fail-closed when forced through unsupported lowerings

backends.gpu_coverage: 0/2 GPU stages active on current host
backends.notes: Plonky3 STARK backend with native field profiles: Goldilocks/BabyBear over FRI and Mersenne31 over Circle PCS. Supports Equal/Boolean constraints, range constraints via bit decomposition (field-safe bit cap), and division via derived quotient columns. implementation_type=native compiled_in=true production_ready=true assurance_lane=native-cryptographic-proof proof_semantics=proof-enforced-lowered-ir aggregation_semantics=single-proof-with-optional-wrapper

backends.gpu_coverage: 0/2 GPU stages active on current host
backends.notes: Plonkish backend implemented with Halo2 (IPA). Supports Equal/Boolean/Range/Div. Range constraints wider than 16 bits are lowered into 16-bit lookup chunks automatically. implementation_type=native compiled_in=true production_ready=true assurance_lane=native-cryptographic-proof proof_semantics=proof-enforced-basic-ir aggregation_semantics=single-proof-only

backends.gpu_coverage: 0/2 GPU stages active on current host
backends.notes: PLONK+KZG on BLS12-381 via PSE halo2 fork. Real BLS12-381 Fr field, SHPLONK multiopen, deterministic trusted setup. Range constraints wider than 16 bits are lowered into 16-bit lookup chunks automatically. implementation_type=native compiled_in=true production_ready=true assurance_lane=native-cryptographic-proof proof_semantics=proof-enforced-basic-ir aggregation_semantics=single-proof-only

frontends.notes: Native Zir source frontend. Tier 1 is total and bounded; Tier 2 preserves advanced ZIR features and fails closed when forced through unsupported lowerings.

ACIR import supports AssertZero, RANGE, AND/XOR (bit-decomposed), native BlackBox constraints (SHA256/Keccak256/Pedersen/Schnorr/ECDSA/Blake2s/RecursiveAggregation marker), plus compatibility hint-backed blackbox metadata for unsupported ops, Directive::ToLeRadix, MemoryInit/MemoryOp (constant and dynamic index via multiplexer), Brillig hints, and multi-function Call inlining.

Circom frontend supports snarkjs-style R1CS JSON import (`constraints`, `nVars`) and descriptor/direct Program passthrough; execution supports native witness-runner descriptors (`witness_runner.kind=snarkjs`) plus command hooks and descriptor witness loading.

Cairo frontend imports Sierra IR (JSON) into ZKF. Supports descriptor-based import with optional build hooks (e.g., `scarb build`), direct ZIR loading for stateful Cairo/StarkNet flows, and descriptor-driven execution via `execute_command`. Native Sierra → constraint translation handles the supported felt252/integer/enum/struct/memory/control-flow subset. Unsupported Sierra libfuncs fail closed during import instead of being silently lowered to fallback BlackBox constraints.
Midnight Compact frontend: imports local compactc 0.30.0 zkir v2.0 circuits directly, auto-discovers contract sidecars when available, and still supports descriptor-based fallback flows.
Halo2 frontend import accepts explicit ZkfHalo2Export schema (`schema=zkf-halo2-export-v1`) via embedded export object or `halo2_export_path`.
Plonky3 AIR frontend import accepts explicit ZkfPlonky3AirExport schema (`schema=zkf-plonky3-air-export-v1`) via embedded export object or `plonky3_air_export_path`.
External-tool frontend for zkVM (SP1, RISC Zero): supports descriptor-based import/execute with toolchain hooks, ELF binary detection, and witness loading.

gadgets.notes: Boolean logic gates (AND, OR, NOT, XOR).
KZG polynomial commitment pairing verification. blackbox_ops=pairing_check
Universal Plonk gate constraint with configurable selectors.
SHA-256 hash function circuit (NIST FIPS 180-4). blackbox_ops=sha256

registry.notes: Local + remote registries with CombinedRegistry fallback, SemVer resolution, and SHA-256 manifest/content integrity checks.
